prompt:
You are building a calendar assistant. Given a list of existing meetings as half-open intervals [start, end) where times are represented as minutes from midnight (0-1440), and a working window [work_start, work_end), return all free (available) time slots within the working window.

Steps to solve:
1. Clip meetings to the working window (ignore parts outside it).
2. Merge all overlapping or adjacent meetings into busy blocks.
3. Return the gaps between busy blocks within the working window.

Return a list of [start, end] pairs sorted by start time. If there are no free slots, return an empty list. If there are no meetings (or none within the window), return the entire working window as one free slot.

Constraints:
- 0 <= work_start < work_end <= 1440
- 0 <= meetings[i][0] < meetings[i][1] <= 1440
- 0 <= len(meetings) <= 10000
- Meetings may overlap, be adjacent, be unsorted, or lie entirely outside the working window.

Expected time complexity: O(n log n) where n = len(meetings).
validation_trace: Verified reference solution against all 11 tests: (1) Two non-overlapping meetings yield 3 free slots. (2) Two overlapping meetings merge into one block with 2 free slots. (3) No meetings returns full window. (4) Full-day meeting returns empty. (5) Meetings outside window are clipped away, returning full window. (6) Adjacent meetings merge (600<=600). (7) Meeting starting at window start leaves gap after. (8) Four overlapping meetings merge into two blocks yielding 3 gaps. (9) Partially outside meetings clip correctly. (10) Unsorted input sorted before merging. (11) Single-minute gap [599,600] correctly detected. All pass.
